items.title: What do Canadian diplomats miss from home?
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>From bagels and poutine to wide open spaces and the people we love: it’s the little things that make Canada home.

In this video, we ask Canada’s diplomats what they miss the most from home.</article>

items.title: 🔥 Canada in the Spotlight as FIFA World Cup Co-Host!
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>As co-host of the FIFA World Cup 2026™, Canada’s message is making headlines around the world.</article>

items.title: RUTFs have been saving lives for 30 years
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>This year, we’re marking 30 years of a life-saving breakthrough.

Ready-to-Use Therapeutic Food (RUTF) has helped millions of severely malnourished children recover and thrive. 

Why does it work? It’s simple, safe, affordable, and easy to use. 

Canada is proud to support UNICEF to deliver RUTF to those who need it most. 

This small sachet still has a big impact 🌍✨ 

#Nutrition #GlobalHealth #SaveLives</article>

items.title: Canada is ready to welcome the world for the FIFA World Cup 2026™!
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content: <article>The Honourable Adam van Koeverden, Secretary of State (Sport) and Canada's FIFA Sherpa and Canada's ambassadors pass the ball to welcome the world for the FIFA World Cup 2026™ #WeAreCanada #WorldFootballDay</article>

items.title: Canada's 2024-2025 International Assistance Report
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Partnership is our greatest multiplier. 🤝🌍 

In a shifting global landscape, Canada is focusing on results.  
Our 2024 to 2025 International Assistance Report shows how 20 federal departments are working together to tackle challenges head-on. 

Why this matters at home:  
✅ Economic security: Helping partners build resilient economies 
✅ Stability: Creating a more predictable world for trade 
✅ Resilience: Smart investments today that prevent the crises of tomorrow 

When we build a safer world, we build a stronger Canada. 

🔗 For more info, click the link ➡️ https://international.canada.ca/en/global-affairs/corporate/reports/international-assistance-report/report-overview-2025</article>

items.title: Episode 2 – Why it matters: Global health
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>We live in a highly connected world. In just days, a disease can spread across borders, turning a local issue into a global challenge. But Canada is taking action. 

By working with experienced partners, Canada is investing in: 

🍎 essential nutrition and clean water 
💉 life-saving immunizations 
🏥 stronger health care systems 
🩺 health worker training 
🤍maternal care support  

Why does this matter for Canadians?

True to its values, Canada strives for a better future for all.  
By caring for the most vulnerable and building resilient communities worldwide, we ensure:  

✅ safer travel for Canadians abroad 
✅ less pressure on international resources 
✅ a stronger, more stable economy here at home   

Making a difference in the world makes a difference for Canadians, too. 

Learn more about our work: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/global_health-sante_mondiale/index.aspx?lang=eng   

#GlobalHealth #Canada #PublicHealth #Innovation #CdnPoli</article>

items.title: How Division Can Be Manufactured
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>That heated debate in your feed? It might be manufactured outrage. Some foreign regimes exploit real tensions, target communities, amplify extremes and weaponize anger to divide us. 
 
Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: Why Critical Voices Can Fall Silent
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Dissenting voices disappearing from your feed? It might not be an accident.  Some foreign regimes silence critics through takedowns, legal threats and intimidation campaigns. 

Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: When Extreme Ideas Seem Normal
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Extreme ideas can be made to seem mainstream. Some foreign regimes amplify fringe views with memes, emotional posts and carefully timed campaigns. 
 
Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: How Your Feed Is Manipulated
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Is what you see in your feed real or rigged? Some foreign regimes exploit algorithms to bury facts and boost lies. 
 
Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: The Illusion of Online Consensus
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Online consensus? It might be an illusion. Some foreign regimes use bots and fake accounts to simulate agreement or stir outrage. 
 
Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: When “Local Voices” Aren’t Local
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>It looks local and sounds independent. But some foreign regimes hide their role by laundering content through fake local outlets and hijacking trusted voices. 
 
Learn the tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: Replacing Facts with Fiction
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>It looks real. It sounds real. But it’s been manipulated. Some foreign regimes tweak real content, by editing footage, altering audio or adding false captions. 
 
Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: When Facts Get Twisted
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Real facts can be twisted. Some foreign regimes reframe genuine events by stripping context, cherry-picking details or combining unrelated content. 
 
Learn their tactics and #ThinkBeforeYouShare 
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng</article>

items.title: How Foreign Information Manipulation Works
items.author: Global Affairs Canada
items.content:
<article>Some foreign regimes twist and distort online content to divide and weaken societies. They go beyond spreading #disinformation, using subtle tactics designed to deceive.
 
See how Canada is taking action: https://www.international.gc.ca/world-monde/issues_development-enjeux_developpement/peace_security-paix_securite/combatt-disinformation-desinformation.aspx?lang=eng

#ThinkBeforeYouShare</article>
